title: Deaths from diabetes, by age
subtitle: Annual number of deaths from diabetes. This includes both type I and type II diabetes.
caption:
Deaths from diabetes, by age

Annual number of deaths from diabetes. This includes both type I and type II diabetes.

This stacked area chart displays the annual number of global deaths from diabetes from 1980 to 2023, broken down by age group. The vertical axis measures the number of deaths, ranging from 0 to 2 million. The total number of deaths shows a continuous upward trend, starting at approximately 500,000 in 1980 and rising to reach the 2 million mark by 2023. The largest portion of deaths throughout the entire period is attributed to the "70+ years old" group, represented by the top brown band, which expands significantly in height over the decades. The "50-69 years old" group, shown in reddish-orange, constitutes the second-largest segment and also demonstrates substantial growth, particularly accelerating after 2010. The "15-49 years old" group forms a thinner green band near the bottom that widens slightly over time. The lowest two categories, "5-14 years old" and "Under-5s," appear as very thin lines at the base of the chart, indicating consistently low numbers relative to the older age groups. By the end of the timeline in 2023, the combined height of the areas reaches the maximum value on the y-axis, driven primarily by the increasing volume of deaths in the two oldest age brackets.
